verses.text: The mighty God summons all the earth, shining from Zion, and 'our God shall come, and shall not keep silence.' The Judge appears in majesty. The God who once seemed silent will surely speak at last.
The mighty God summons all the earth, shining from Zion, and 'our God shall come, and shall not keep silence.' The Judge appears in majesty. The God who once seemed silent will surely speak at last.
The mighty God summons all the earth, shining from Zion, and 'our God shall come, and shall not keep silence.' The Judge appears in majesty. The God who once seemed silent will surely speak at last.
He calls heaven and earth to witness as He judges His people, and 'the heavens shall declare his righteousness.' The court is convened. God's judgment of His own is open, just, and sure.
He calls heaven and earth to witness as He judges His people, and 'the heavens shall declare his righteousness.' The court is convened. God's judgment of His own is open, just, and sure.
He calls heaven and earth to witness as He judges His people, and 'the heavens shall declare his righteousness.' The court is convened. God's judgment of His own is open, just, and sure.
'Hear, O my people,' He says; He does not rebuke them for lack of sacrifices, which they brought in plenty. Their ritual was not the problem. God looks past the outward offering to the heart.
'Hear, O my people,' He says; He does not rebuke them for lack of sacrifices, which they brought in plenty. Their ritual was not the problem. God looks past the outward offering to the heart.

He needs no bullock or goat, for every beast and 'the cattle upon a thousand hills' are His; were He hungry, He would not tell them. God lacks nothing we could give. Worship adds nothing to Him; it is for our good, not His supply.

He needs no bullock or goat, for every beast and 'the cattle upon a thousand hills' are His; were He hungry, He would not tell them. God lacks nothing we could give. Worship adds nothing to Him; it is for our good, not His supply.

Rather, 'Offer unto God thanksgiving,' and 'call upon me in the day of trouble: I will deliver thee.' Grateful trust is the worship He seeks. He asks not gifts He needs not, but the praise and prayer of dependent hearts.

Rather, 'Offer unto God thanksgiving,' and 'call upon me in the day of trouble: I will deliver thee.' Grateful trust is the worship He seeks. He asks not gifts He needs not, but the praise and prayer of dependent hearts.

To the wicked God says, what right have they to recite His statutes when they hate instruction and cast His words behind them? Profession without obedience is empty. God will not be honoured by lips while the life defies Him.

To the wicked God says, what right have they to recite His statutes when they hate instruction and cast His words behind them? Profession without obedience is empty. God will not be honoured by lips while the life defies Him.
They side with thieves and adulterers, give their mouth to evil, and slander their own brother. Their deeds expose their hearts. A religion that lives in open sin is a contradiction God condemns.
They side with thieves and adulterers, give their mouth to evil, and slander their own brother. Their deeds expose their hearts. A religion that lives in open sin is a contradiction God condemns.
They side with thieves and adulterers, give their mouth to evil, and slander their own brother. Their deeds expose their hearts. A religion that lives in open sin is a contradiction God condemns.

God kept silence and they thought Him like themselves, but He will reprove them; 'whoso offereth praise glorifieth me,' and to the upright He shows His salvation. His patience is not approval. True worship is a thankful, ordered life that ends in seeing God's salvation.
